steps.ordered: CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

ensemble.other_hypothesis: Crohn's/IBD flare (given immunosuppression on Enbrel and methotrexate), mesenteric adenitis, right-sided colitis, psoas abscess, or right ovarian/adnexal pathology (though hysterectomy noted, ovaries may remain)
ensemble.information_gap: The anatomic source and nature of the RLQ pain cannot be determined from history, exam, labs, and plain chest film alone — specifically whether there is appendiceal inflammation, colonic/diverticular disease, or an alternative intra-abdominal process in an immunosuppressed host whose inflammatory response (WBC 6.5, neutrophilia 79.3%) may be blunted
ensemble.expected_finding: Periappendiceal fat stranding with a dilated, non-compressible appendix (appendicitis), or alternatively colonic wall thickening, pericolic fat stranding, or an abscess/phlegmon in the RLQ
ensemble.appropriateness_reason: RLQ tenderness with neutrophilia in an immunosuppressed patient (Enbrel, methotrexate) who cannot mount a robust leukocytosis warrants cross-sectional imaging to identify the anatomic source and exclude surgical emergency before any intervention.
grounding: History: 'it got acutely worse and localized to the RLQ'
History: 'psoriatic arthritis on enbrel and methotrexate'
Laboratory tests: 'White Blood Cells: 6.5 K/uL' and 'Neutrophils: 79.3 %'
Prior imaging: 'no focal consolidation, effusion, or pneumothorax... No free air below the right hemidiaphragm'
ensemble.reasoning: This immunosuppressed patient (Enbrel + methotrexate) presents with acute RLQ pain and neutrophilia but a normal total WBC, a pattern consistent with a blunted inflammatory response that makes clinical exam and labs unreliable for ruling out appendicitis or other surgical pathology. The chest X-ray excluded free air and pulmonary causes, but the intra-abdominal source remains undefined. CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast is the logical next step to directly visualize the appendix, colon, and surrounding structures and determine whether urgent surgical or medical management is needed.

representative_ex_ante.appropriateness_reason: RLQ tenderness with neutrophilia in an immunosuppressed patient (Enbrel, methotrexate) who cannot mount a robust leukocytosis warrants cross-sectional imaging to identify the anatomic source and exclude surgical emergency before any intervention.

History: 'psoriatic arthritis on enbrel and methotrexate'
Laboratory tests: 'White Blood Cells: 6.5 K/uL' and 'Neutrophils: 79.3 %'
Prior imaging: 'no focal consolidation, effusion, or pneumothorax... No free air below the right hemidiaphragm'

The chest X-ray excluded free air and pulmonary causes, but the intra-abdominal source remains undefined. CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast is the logical next step to directly visualize the appendix, colon, and surrounding structures and determine whether urgent surgical or medical management is needed.

vindication.actual_finding: A retrocecal appendix measuring 1 cm in diameter, fluid-filled with mucosal enhancement and surrounding fat stranding, most compatible with acute appendicitis.
vindication.relation_to_expectation: The CT directly demonstrated the periappendiceal fat stranding and dilated, non-compressible (fluid-filled) appendix that the physician specifically listed as the primary expected finding for appendicitis.
